parentQuestion: A 70-year-old man with chronic kidney disease has persistently high blood calciu...

scaffolds.q: Which cells break down bone to release calcium into the bloodstream?
scaffolds.explain: Osteoclasts are bone-destroying cells that resorb bone and release calcium into the blood. They're regulated by parathyroid hormone (PTH) as part of calcium homeostasis. The suffix '-clast' means 'breaker.' Know your demolition crew, darling.

scaffolds.q: Parathyroid hormone (PTH) is secreted when blood calcium is:
scaffolds.explain: PTH is secreted when blood calcium drops below normal. PTH then acts on bone (stimulates osteoclasts to release calcium), kidney (increases calcium reabsorption), and indirectly on intestine (via vitamin D activation) to RAISE blood calcium. Negative feedback loop, love.
